name: Richie Powell
abstract: Richard Powell (September 5, 1931 – June 26, 1956) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and arranger. He was not assisted in his musical development by Bud, his older and better known brother, but both played predominantly in the bebop style.

concerts.label: Jazz and Variety Concert to Benefit The Lighthouse

works.t: Anything Goes: I Get a Kick Out of You

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: We had a thing man, there was group -- the success of the group was absolutely fantastic... And we came east and that’s the first time Buddy turned left on me... fortunately I got Jerome, your boy, Jerome filled in for me for that week... Richie and Clifford are gone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: Clifford Brown and Richie Powell got killed in a car accident, so – I think on the Pennsylvania Turnpike... So that left Max and George Morrow, the bass player. So Donald Byrd, Sonny Rollins and myself, we joined Max’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: I had heard what a great trumpet player he was, and I had hardly heard the band -- I didn't have access to no records, or recordings of the Max Roach Quintet: Clifford and Harold Land and Richie Powell and George Morrow.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: And Richie and Clifford and Richie’s wife came in. And it was so sad. I think that’s the night they were killed driving back to New York, you know. So that was… And I know it was a huge blow to Sonny so…
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Marshall
about_quotes.evidence: And then after that I was on a quest. I knew I had to leave Springfield and go to New York. Around that same time was when Clifford Brown and Richie Powell died.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Marshall interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: We all hugged them and told them so long, and that’s when they were on the highway and got killed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
